title: trifolium campestre var. majus
intro: Trifolium campestre var. majus, also known as the large hop clover, is a perennial legume native to Europe and western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The large hop clover has been traditionally used to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. It can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or dried herb, and is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional remedies.
